etymology.notes: gindirîn, Chyet 213: to roll, tumble, overturn;

Preterit.turoyo: ‘Lu+čalkoyo qṭile Osḳe, qṭile Osḳe. Ḥsayno azzé lu+Šamʕən du+Išḳo, azzele lu+Šamʕən du+Išḳo, lu+čalkoyo qṭile háwoste. Mḥele i+tfәnge bi+tḥelayḏe w mgandaḷḷe háwoste, qṭile háwoste’.
translations:
The
			Yezidi killed Oske, he killed Oske. Ḥәsayno went to Šamʕən
			of Išḳo, he went to Šamʕən of Išḳo, the Yezidi killed
			this one too. He fired a gun into his side and he rolled, this
			one, he killed this one.
(Ilyas p.c.) I killed two men today lit. I made them roll.

Participle_Passive.turoyo: kət-li mede mgandəro, w kət-li mede yarixo 776; prs 255/30, 31;
translations:
ich
			weiss etwas rundes und weiss etwas langes
